campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss changes to business license tax rates in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented an ordinance that would update how much local businesses pay in annual license taxes. The council discussed the proposal and is expected to vote on whether to approve these new tax rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate changes
Annual tax update for local businesses
Finance department presentation on new rates
Council deliberation and voting on the ordinance

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council introduced a new ordinance and decided to skip the usual first reading process to move things faster. The ordinance is set to be voted on and officially adopted at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next regular meeting
First reading requirement waived to streamline process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and reviewed the town's finances for February. The council authorized the town manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services, though the specific details of these projects appear incomplete in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed town finances and investment report for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with multiple companies
Approved professional services agreements (details unclear)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 22, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and organized the agendas for two upcoming meetings (April 28 and May 5), decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked if any agenda items needed special notice requirements. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized April 28 meeting agenda and added any new items
Organized May 5 draft agenda with new business items
Set specific times for certain agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public records from April 9-16, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what major projects and improvements the city wants to fund. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and improvement projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Planning session for next year's capital spending

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting at 6 PM to discuss confidential matters (the specific topics weren't disclosed to the public), followed by a regular council meeting at 7 PM that included recognizing award winners and other business.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not publicly disclosed)
Recognition of award winners
Regular council business and formal proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, where they discussed two legal cases that could potentially cost the city money. They also gave recognition to the Administrative Services Department for their work on budgeting, finance, and purchasing. The meeting included a public comment period where residents could speak about any city matters.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits that pose financial risk to the city
Recognition of Administrative Services Department staff
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing temporary large-scale signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new labor contract with the city's firefighters through 2029. One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (2026-2029)
Staff withdrew one item from the agenda

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve the city's next budget and fee updates for the 2026-27 fiscal year. The council also discussed tax rules, heard about Santa Clara County's plan to address homelessness in the community, and handled routine meeting business like approving past meeting minutes.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
Updates to city user fees and charges

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that was already approved for the property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow the removal of a protected tree on this property.
keyTopics: 751 Waverley Street tree removal permit under review
Protected tree removal decision being reconsidered
Director's Review process for planning approval
